goal: Build the demo_ur10_conveyor canonical: UR10 + Cortex behavior tree picks cubes off a moving conveyor (vs CP-72's stationary feeder) and drops in a bin. Realizes #33 demo_ur10_conveyor — the canonical Isaac Sim 5.x example for UR10 + Cortex + active conveyor.
thoughts:
Six demo_ur10_conveyor specifics for CP-73 (extends CP-72 with active conveyor instead of stationary feeder):
1) UR10 base on table at (0, 0, 0.75) — same layout as CP-71/72.
2) ConveyorBelt with surface_velocity=0.2 m/s (matches Franka CP-01 baseline) — cubes flow through pick zone.
3) 4 cubes spawned along conveyor x axis; flow into UR10's pick window. Cortex motion adapts to moving cubes (RmpFlow's cube_tracking mode internally).
4) setup_cortex_behavior wraps the UR10 with CortexUr10 + obstacle registration. This is exactly what the Isaac Sim demo_ur10_conveyor.py example does.
5) target_source='curobo' + robot_family='ur10' for actual motion planning. Cortex provides obstacle awareness; cuRobo handles trajectory.
6) Realizes #33 demo_ur10_conveyor — the canonical UR10+Cortex example. With CP-72 (stationary stacking) and CP-71 (dispenser fill) this completes the UR10 + Cortex research scenario triad.
code:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# UR10 base on table
import_robot(file_path="UR10", format="asset_library", dest_path="/World/UR10")
teleport_prim(prim_path="/World/UR10", position=[0, 0, 0.75])

# Active conveyor (v=0.2 m/s) — cubes flow into pick zone
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[-0.5, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[2.5, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 4 cubes upstream of pick zone
for i, x in enumerate([-1.6, -1.4, -1.2, -1.0]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# Output bin
create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[0.5, -0.3, 0.75], size=[0.30, 0.30, 0.15])

# Pick sensor at conveyor pick zone
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Cortex behavior wrapper for UR10 (matches isaacsim.cortex demo pattern)
setup_cortex_behavior(
    robot_path="/World/UR10",
    robot_kind="ur10",
    obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Bin"],
)

# UR10 cuRobo controller — single drop target (Bin)
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/UR10",
    robot_family="ur10",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/Bin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-73 swaps CP-72's stationary feeder for an active conveyor (v=0.2 m/s). Realizes #33 demo_ur10_conveyor — the canonical Isaac Sim 5.x UR10+Cortex demo. With CP-71 (bin filling) + CP-72 (vertical stacking) + CP-73 (moving conveyor), the UR10+Cortex research scenario triad is complete.
failure_modes: Belt-pause coordination: native handler pauses belt during pick to settle physics. cuRobo handler does the same via _pause_belt/_resume_belt. Cortex layer should not interfere.
Pick window: with belt v=0.2 m/s and ~1m of conveyor lead-in, cubes arrive at pick zone every ~5s. UR10 cycle time must beat that or backlog forms.
Same UR10 surface-gripper limitation as CP-69 — function-gate deferred.
Verifier looks for surfaceVelocity > 0 — already provided.
